target_desc: examples/binder.yaml — de-novo protein binder vs chain A of PDB 7ROA (protein-anything protocol)
reference_impl: official BoltzGen (HannesStark/boltzgen, torch + pytorch-lightning, CUDA GPU)
weights: HuggingFace boltzgen/boltzgen-1, snapshot c1be29e1f82ffcc72264f64b993c43fb4e0d17f0, boltzgen1_diverse.ckpt (design) + boltz2_conf_final.ckpt (design_folding refold). Public, unauthenticated pull.
command: boltzgen run binder.yaml --output <out> --num_designs 8 --budget 8 --protocol protein-anything --devices 1 --use_kernels auto --no_subprocess   (design_folding + analysis need the folding step's fold_out_npz, so the production run uses the full default step set: design, inverse_folding, folding, design_folding, analysis, filtering; no --seed flag — BoltzGen has none, each invocation is an independent draw, matching the device discipline)

settings.seed: none — BoltzGen exposes no --seed flag; each process is an independent draw (same as the device leg)
settings.target: examples/binder.yaml (binder vs chain A of 7ROA, length sampled 80..120 per design)

device_vs_reference_parity.verdict: device designability meets-or-exceeds the reference (device <=2A pass-rate 93.75% vs reference 68.75%; device median scRMSD 0.78 A vs reference 1.05 A). The device sits on the favorable side of the reference's own batch-to-band spread (reference batch_a 75% vs batch_b 62.5% <=2A, a 12.5pp band at n=8). The port does not regress designability; if anything it is marginally more designable on this target. No 1:1 output correspondence (BoltzGen designs new sequences), so parity is in the designability distribution, not a per-design RMSD.
device_vs_reference_parity.caveat: n=16 per side is small; the reference's own two batches are 12.5pp apart on the <=2A bar, so part of the device-reference gap is sampling noise. The direction (device >= reference) is consistent across both batch pairs.

provenance: Generated 2026-07-13 on a rented vast.ai on-demand RTX 3090 (instance 44721818, ~$0.298/hr on-demand). Two independent n=8 invocations of the official BoltzGen CLI form the reference designability distribution (the R floor analog). Settings match the device design.yaml defaults exactly (recycling_steps=3, sampling_steps=500, diffusion_samples=1, bf16-mixed).
hardware_invariance: This is the GPU reference leg. The same official BoltzGen has no CPU path (it calls torch.cuda.get_device_capability() unconditionally), so the reference is GPU-only by construction; the CPU-vs-GPU equivalence question does not apply to BoltzGen (see the Boltz-2 / Protenix-v2 sections of docs/pharma-benchmark.md for the hardware-invariance check on models that DO have a CPU path).
invalidation_rule: Regenerate this fixture ONLY when the pinned reference_version/weights snapshot changes or the settings above change. For any other change (device seeds, device code, release tag) the fixture is reused as-is and only the device side re-runs.
